content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree consisting of NNN nodes and N−1N-1N−1 edges. The iii-th node has value AiA_iAi​.

Let d(x,y)d(x, y)d(x,y) denote the number of edges on the (unique) shortest path between xxx and yyy.

For each node u (1≤u≤N)u \ (1 \leq u \leq N)u (1≤u≤N), compute the following quantity:

∑i=1N⌊Ai2d(u,i)⌋\sum_{i=1}^N \left \lfloor \frac{A_i}{2^{d(u, i)}} \right\rfloori=1∑N​⌊2d(u,i)Ai​​⌋Here, ⌊ ⌋\left\lfloor \ \right\rfloor⌊ ⌋ denotes the floor function.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of nodes in the tree.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers, the iii-th of which denotes the answer for node iii.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1045\cdot 10^45⋅104.
